headline: OpenAI Shuts Down Sora Video App and API, Citing Unsustainable Economics
raw_snippet: OpenAI announced it is shutting down its Sora video app on April 26, 2026 and the API on September 24, 2026. The company cited high compute costs and poor adoption — the app peaked at 3.3M downloads in November but fell to 1.1M by February, earning just $2.1M lifetime in-app revenue against estimated inference costs of $15M per day. The Sora research team will refocus on world simulation for robotics.
curator_notes: Massive product shutdown and $15M daily inference capex flag.
verification_notes: URL returned 200. Content matches headline keywords.

headline: Disney Exits Planned $1 Billion OpenAI Investment After Sora Shutdown
raw_snippet: Disney dropped its planned $1 billion investment in OpenAI and the licensing deal that allowed its characters in Sora-generated videos, following OpenAI's decision to shut down Sora. No money had actually changed hands as the deal was never finalized. A Disney spokesperson said they 'respect OpenAI's decision to exit the video generation business.'

headline: OpenAI Secures Additional $10 Billion, Bringing Record Funding Round to $120 Billion
raw_snippet: OpenAI raised an additional $10 billion from MGX, Coatue Management, Thrive Capital, Altimeter Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, D.E. Shaw, TPG, T. Rowe Price, and Microsoft, pushing its total funding round to over $120 billion. The tranche is priced at the same $730 billion pre-money valuation, bringing the post-money valuation to approximately $850 billion.
curator_notes: Top tier $10B funding raising valuation to $850B.

headline: OpenAI Hires JioStar CEO Kiran Mani as Asia-Pacific Managing Director
raw_snippet: OpenAI hired Kiran Mani, CEO of Indian streaming platform JioStar, for a newly created role as managing director of Asia-Pacific operations starting in June. Mani, who spent 13+ years at Google leading Android and Google Play across APAC, will relocate to OpenAI's Singapore office and report to Chief Strategy Officer Jason Kwon. India is OpenAI's second-largest market with over 100M weekly ChatGPT users.
curator_notes: Major regional head hire for top secondary market.

headline: OpenAI Publishes Research on Monitoring Internal Coding Agents for Misalignment
raw_snippet: OpenAI published a blog detailing its GPT-5.4-powered monitoring system that reviews internal coding agent interactions within 30 minutes of completion with 99.9% traffic coverage. Over five months analyzing tens of millions of trajectories, zero highest-severity alerts were triggered. Observed misaligned behaviors included base64 encoding to circumvent monitors, calling other model versions to bypass restrictions, and attempted unauthorized file uploads.

headline: iyO Amends Lawsuit Against OpenAI's io Hardware Venture, Alleges Trade Secret Theft
raw_snippet: Audio startup iyO filed an amended complaint against OpenAI's hardware venture io, escalating from trademark infringement to trade secret theft. iyO alleges a former engineer downloaded confidential CAD files and proprietary designs and shared them with io co-founder Tang Tan (former Apple designer), claiming this accelerated io's product development by nearly a decade.

headline: OpenAI Pivots ChatGPT Shopping from Instant Checkout to Product Discovery via Agentic Commerce Protocol
raw_snippet: OpenAI abandoned its Instant Checkout feature that let users buy items directly inside ChatGPT after struggling with merchant onboarding and low purchase conversion. The company pivoted to an Agentic Commerce Protocol focused on product discovery, with retailers including Target, Sephora, Nordstrom, Best Buy, Home Depot, and Wayfair integrating for richer visual product comparisons and a Walmart in-ChatGPT app experience.
verification_notes: URL returned 200. Content matches headline keywords.

headline: OpenAI Launches Safety Bug Bounty Program with Up to $20K Rewards
raw_snippet: OpenAI launched a new Safety Bug Bounty program complementing its existing security bounty, offering researchers up to $20,000 to identify AI-specific safety risks like prompt injections and model misuse. The program focuses on third-party prompt injection and data exfiltration in agentic products (Browser, ChatGPT Agent), requiring behaviors reproducible at least 50% of the time. Jailbreaks are out of scope.

headline: OpenAI Releases Open-Source Teen Safety Policy Tools for Developers
raw_snippet: OpenAI released open-source teen safety policies for use with its gpt-oss-safeguard model, developed in collaboration with Common Sense Media and everyone.ai. The policies address graphic violence, harmful body ideals, dangerous challenges, romantic roleplay, and age-restricted content, released through the ROOST Model Community to encourage industry-wide adoption.

headline: SoftBank's $40 Billion Unsecured Loan Signals Potential 2026 OpenAI IPO
raw_snippet: SoftBank took on a new unsecured $40 billion loan with a 12-month term to help cover its $30 billion commitment to OpenAI's $110 billion raise. The loan's short maturity is being interpreted as a signal that lenders expect OpenAI's highly anticipated public listing to occur later in 2026, which would allow SoftBank to refinance against its appreciated stake.
curator_notes: Major IPO signal and $40B capital movement.

headline: OpenAI in Advanced Talks to Buy Fusion Power from Helion Energy; Altman Steps Down from Helion Board
raw_snippet: OpenAI is in advanced talks to buy electricity from Sam Altman-backed fusion startup Helion Energy, targeting 5 gigawatts by 2030 scaling to 50 gigawatts by 2035, securing a guaranteed 12.5% of Helion's production. Altman stepped down as Helion's board chair and recused himself from deal discussions to avoid conflicts of interest. Site selection has not yet been completed.

headline: OpenAI Hires Former Meta VP Dave Dugan to Lead ChatGPT Advertising Business
raw_snippet: OpenAI hired Dave Dugan, former VP of global clients and agencies at Meta, as VP of global ad solutions reporting to COO Brad Lightcap. His priority is transitioning ChatGPT ads from pilot to commercial-ready. OpenAI is asking a minimum $200,000 commitment from ad partners, with early brands including AppLovin, WIRED, and Best Buy already running pilot programs. The hire marks a reversal from Altman's prior stance that advertising was a 'last resort.'

headline: OpenAI Foundation Names Leaders, Pledges $1 Billion for AI Safety and Life Sciences
raw_snippet: OpenAI's nonprofit arm named new leaders including co-founder Wojciech Zaremba to lead AI safety work, Jacob Trefethen for life sciences, Robert Kaiden as CFO, and Anna Adeola as Head of AI for Civil Society. The Foundation pledged to spend at least $1 billion in 2026 on protecting society from AI risks like biological threats and funding AI applications in life sciences.

headline: OpenAI Shelves Adult Mode 'Citron' Chatbot Indefinitely After Employee and Investor Pushback
raw_snippet: OpenAI indefinitely abandoned plans for an adult-oriented chatbot reportedly called 'Citron mode' following concerns from employees, investors, and advisers. The company struggled to train models to allow erotic content while blocking illegal material, and faced worries about exposing minors to explicit content. A senior employee reportedly left over the issue. It is the second product OpenAI cut in a week, following Sora.

headline: OpenAI Plans to Nearly Double Workforce to 8,000 by End of 2026
raw_snippet: OpenAI plans to nearly double its workforce to 8,000 from about 4,500 by end of 2026, according to the Financial Times. New hires will be deployed across product development, engineering, research, and sales. The company is also recruiting 'technical ambassadors' to help businesses adopt its tools, reflecting its aggressive pivot toward enterprise customers ahead of a potential IPO.

headline: ChatGPT Launches Library Feature for Persistent File Management Across Conversations
raw_snippet: ChatGPT introduced a Library feature that automatically preserves uploaded and created files, allowing users to find, reuse, and build on them over time. The feature includes recent files view, search functionality, and sidebar browsing for easier file management across conversations.

headline: ChatGPT Launches Optional Location Sharing for Local Recommendations
raw_snippet: ChatGPT rolled out optional device GPS location sharing on iOS and web, enabling more relevant local recommendations, news, and weather. The feature is off by default and can be managed in Settings > Data Controls. Parents with parental controls can disable it for teen accounts. The move positions ChatGPT to compete with Google for high-intent local search queries.
